results.content:
## AI Analysis

📝 Summary

Ellis is an AI notetaker for in-person meetings that records audio using only an iPhone or Apple Watch. It delivers clean transcripts with speaker identification and enables natural language queries about decisions, missed details, or meeting sentiment. It solves key pain points like distraction from manual note-taking, incomplete records, and post-meeting transcription effort. The value proposition is effortless, hardware-free capture and intelligent insights for face-to-face conversations, boosting productivity while keeping meetings natural.

## AI Analysis

📝 Summary [...] ⚔️ Competition

Medium. Direct competitors: 1. Otter.ai (otter.ai), 2. Fireflies.ai (fireflies.ai), 3. Limitless (limitless.ai), 4. Mem (mem.ai), 5. Notta (notta.ai). Ellis advantages: pure in-person focus with zero extra hardware, seamless iPhone/Apple Watch experience, and conversational querying. Disadvantages: Apple-only ecosystem limits reach, faces accuracy challenges vs established players with more training data, and lacks some virtual meeting integrations competitors offer.

# Ellis — AI notes for in-person meetings
## Fox SaaS Solutions
1 subscribers

### Description
16 views
Posted: 8 Jul 2026
AI notes for in-person meetings
Ellis is an AI notetaker for in-person meetings. Record your meeting on iPhone or Apple Watch, get a clean transcript with each speaker identified, then ask anything — what was decided, what you missed, how it went. No laptop, no extra hardware.

This product launched without a demo video, so we made one for free.
Make your own launch video in about 30 seconds — paste your site at 
ellis.coach [...] ### Transcript:
Alice, the note taker for real life meetings. Record the room. Alice hands back a clean transcript with each speaker identified. Your best meetings happen across a table, not over a screen. Alice was built for those. Hit record on your iPhone or Apple Watch. Coffee meetups, sales calls, doctor visits, interviews. No laptop, no extra hardware. Record it, get a clean transcript, then ask anything. What was decided, what you missed, how it went. Walk into your next meeting and just be present. Alice remembers the rest.

results.title: The smartest meeting tool you might not have heard of | Samuel Ellis
results.content: 🕒 𝐖𝐨𝐫𝐤𝐞𝐫𝐬 𝐧𝐨𝐰 𝐬𝐩𝐞𝐧𝐝 4.3 𝐡𝐨𝐮𝐫𝐬 𝐚 𝐰𝐞𝐞𝐤 𝐢𝐧 𝐦𝐞𝐞𝐭𝐢𝐧𝐠𝐬 — almost as much time as they spend with friends. Somewhere along the way, 𝐰𝐨𝐫𝐤 𝐭𝐮𝐫𝐧𝐞𝐝 𝐢𝐧𝐭𝐨 𝐬𝐨𝐜𝐢𝐚𝐥 𝐡𝐨𝐮𝐫. And here’s the twist: nearly 𝐡𝐚𝐥𝐟 𝐨𝐟 𝐞𝐦𝐩𝐥𝐨𝐲𝐞𝐞𝐬 𝐡𝐚𝐯𝐞 𝐬𝐞𝐞𝐧 𝐀𝐈 𝐛𝐨𝐭𝐬 𝐚𝐭𝐭𝐞𝐧𝐝 𝐦𝐞𝐞𝐭𝐢𝐧𝐠𝐬 𝐨𝐧 𝐭𝐡𝐞𝐢𝐫 𝐛𝐞𝐡𝐚𝐥𝐟. Honestly? I don’t blame them. When 77% 𝐨𝐟 𝐦𝐞𝐞𝐭𝐢𝐧𝐠𝐬 𝐨𝐧𝐥𝐲 𝐥𝐞𝐚𝐝 𝐭𝐨 𝐬𝐜𝐡𝐞𝐝𝐮𝐥𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐦𝐨𝐫𝐞 𝐦𝐞𝐞𝐭𝐢𝐧𝐠𝐬, something is clearly broken. 📊 The numbers say it all: • 40% find AI stand-ins rude • 46% have witnessed colleagues using AI assistants • 𝐌𝐞𝐞𝐭𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐨𝐯𝐞𝐫𝐥𝐨𝐚𝐝 is destroying work-life balance But there’s another side to this story. Companies that 𝑑𝑜 use AI meeting tools report 30% 𝐥𝐞𝐬𝐬 𝐭𝐢𝐦𝐞 𝐬𝐩𝐞𝐧𝐭 𝐨𝐧 𝐩𝐨𝐬𝐭-𝐦𝐞𝐞𝐭𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐭𝐚𝐬𝐤𝐬. AI now handles 𝐭𝐫𝐚𝐧𝐬𝐜𝐫𝐢𝐩𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧𝐬, 𝐬𝐮𝐦𝐦𝐚𝐫𝐢𝐞𝐬, 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐚𝐜𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐢𝐭𝐞𝐦𝐬 — freeing humans for actual thinking. So maybe the problem isn’t meetings [...] The most dangerous part of any meeting is the 10 minutes after it ends. Momentum is high, but action items are scattered across notes. Good ideas get lost. Dropped balls become inevitable. Here's a simple AI-powered system to turn every meeting transcript into a productive outcome. The Playbook: Meeting Notes → Actions 1. Record your meetings and get an automatic transcript. 2. Paste the full transcript into your AI tool. 3. Add this prompt at the end: "Act as my executive assistant. Review the meeting transcript above and provide the following in a clean, scannable format: 1. A one-paragraph executive summary of the key discussion points and final decisions. 2. A bulleted list of all specific action items, with the name of the person assigned to each one. 3. A list of any key deadlines [...] Salesforce report 30% time savings on post-meeting tasks. That's significant. The real issue isn't the bots. It's our broken meeting culture. When people choose AI over human presence, that's a signal. We're drowning in discussion but starving for decision-making. Maybe it's time to fix the meetings instead of just automating attendance. What's your take? Are AI meeting assistants a productivity solution or a symptom of deeper workplace problems? #AI #FutureOfWork #Productivity 𝐒𝐨𝐮𝐫𝐜𝐞:

results.title: Best AI Notetakers for In Person Meeting in 2025 - Jamie AI
results.content:
Current AI note-taking tools work poorly for in-person meetings. Most apps focus on online calls, require expensive hardware, or force you to take notes manually. These solutions fail in real meeting rooms.
 People need an AI notetaker that works during face-to-face meetings. The tool should create accurate transcripts with speaker identification, generate summaries with clear decisions and action items, protect privacy, record offline and in-person, allow quick searches, and connect to existing workflows. [...] For many teams I work with, the biggest problem with AI notes isn't the technology - it's trust. A visible meeting bot makes people nervous (and honestly, it's kind of awkward), and it's hard to know where audio goes, how long it stays there, and whether it's used to train someone else's model. In finance, healthcare, or legal work, this feels impossible to approve, even if the notes would help. [...] When I can't remember where a decision was made, I waste so much time hunting. I might skim three different transcripts, search through folders, or ask colleagues, "Do you remember which workshop we talked about this in?" If I run many client sessions or internal workshops, this hunt can stall follow-ups and make me feel like I'm always re-discovering work I already did (which is super frustrating).

results.title: AI Notetakers Could Mean We Don't Have to Attend As Many Work Meetings - Business Insider
results.content:
White said bosses could ask AI for instances in which a meeting was positive or when participants grew frustrated. A search might take the form of, "Give me a pricing discussion that didn't go well," he said. That goes well beyond parsing a transcript for the word "price," he added.

"The tech is finally there, and it's really good," he said.

## An interview with AI

AI could also help document meetings with prospective employees, said Alan Price, the global head of talent acquisition at Deel, a global human resources company that helps employers hire abroad. Price told BI that Deel had begun using AI meeting tools to reduce the time and personnel needed to hire for roles like customer service. [...] Softeye's plan, Sejnowski said, is to have glasses that constantly recognize objects and people around the wearer and provide related information. He said they would also take snapshots and store them, along with the time they were taken. That would make it possible, he said, to reconstruct where a user was — and rely on the AI assistant.

"You can ask it questions," Sejnowski said. "Did I promise anything to this person?"

## Highlight reels of meetings

Richard White, like so many other desk workers, found himself stuck on endless Zoom calls during the pandemic.

He found it frustrating to take notes, jump to another call, and have little time to clean up his takeaways in between. Plus, White said, even good notes weren't always reliable after too long.
